osteopathy.title: Osteopathy
osteopathy.intro: Osteopathy treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch is a regulated allied health service for musculoskeletal conditions — back and neck pain, headaches and migraines, joint pain and arthritis, posture imbalances, muscle tightness, pregnancy-related pain, sports injuries, and growth pain in children. We see patients of all ages, from infants to seniors. ACC-registered, no referral needed. Osteopathy is a regulated allied health profession specialising in musculoskeletal health. Our Hillmorton clinic serves patients across Halswell, Wigram, Addington, Cashmere and surrounding Christchurch suburbs.

specialties.title: Paediatric Osteopathy
specialties.description: Osteopathy is safe and effective for babies, toddlers, and children of all ages. Using gentle, non-invasive techniques, we can help with birth-related discomforts, colic, feeding difficulties, sleep disturbances, growing pains, and postural development. Early treatment supports healthy growth and addresses issues before they become chronic.
specialties.alt: Osteopath performing a gentle paediatric assessment on a baby resting on a treatment table in a bright clinic room

specialties.title: Pregnancy Related Pain
specialties.description: Pregnancy places significant demands on the body as it adapts to support new life. Osteopathic treatment can relieve lower back and pelvic pain, sciatica, rib discomfort, and postural strain — with a gentle approach that is safe throughout all trimesters and supports both mother and baby's wellbeing.
specialties.alt: Pregnant patient seated on a sofa during a consultation with a clinician taking notes on a clipboard

specialties.title: Elderly Patients
specialties.description: As we age, joint stiffness, arthritis, reduced mobility and balance issues can affect quality of life. Osteopathic care for older adults uses gentle techniques to improve movement, reduce pain, and support independence — helping manage chronic conditions and maintain an active, fulfilling lifestyle.
specialties.alt: Osteopath performing a gentle shoulder mobilisation on a seated elderly male patient in a bright clinic treatment room

specialties.title: Sport Injury
specialties.description: Whether you're a weekend warrior or a competitive athlete, osteopathy can help you recover faster and prevent future injuries. We treat sprains, strains, shoulder and knee pain, and overuse injuries — combining hands-on treatment with personalised rehabilitation advice to get you back to doing what you love.
specialties.alt: Two athletes performing paired lunge stretches outdoors at a waterfront calisthenics training area

faq.question: What conditions can osteopathy treat?
faq.answer: Osteopathy can help with back and neck pain, headaches and migraines, joint pain and arthritis, posture imbalances, muscle tightness, pregnancy-related pain, sports injuries, and growth or developmental issues in children. It's suitable for patients of all ages, from infants to seniors.

faq.question: Is osteopathy covered by ACC?
faq.answer: Yes. Our osteopaths are ACC-registered providers, so treatment for an accident-related injury is subsidised by ACC. If you haven't lodged a claim yet, we can help you do so at your first appointment — no referral needed.

faq.question: Is osteopathy safe during pregnancy?
faq.answer: Yes. Osteopathic treatment uses gentle techniques that are safe throughout all trimesters. It can relieve lower back and pelvic pain, sciatica, rib discomfort, and postural strain as your body adapts to support your pregnancy.

faq.question: Can osteopaths treat babies and children?
faq.answer: Yes — osteopathy is safe and effective for babies, toddlers, and children of all ages. We use gentle, non-invasive techniques adapted for younger patients to help with birth-related discomforts, colic, feeding difficulties, sleep disturbances, growing pains, and postural development.

acupuncture.title: Acupuncture & Dry Needling
acupuncture.intro: Acupuncture and dry needling at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch bring two complementary traditions together under one roof: Traditional Chinese Medicine and Western medical acupuncture. We treat chronic and acute pain, headaches and migraines, women's health concerns including fertility and menopause, sleep, stress, digestion, and sports-injury recovery. No referral needed — you can book either practitioner directly. Min Jin leads our Traditional Chinese Medicine practice — she is registered with the Chinese Medicine Council of New Zealand and works across women's health, fertility, sleep, stress, digestion, and chronic pain through a TCM constitutional lens; Min's sessions are private. Nina Hu leads our Western medical acupuncture and dry needling — she holds a Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture) and integrates fine-needle technique with osteopathic treatment for trigger-point release, musculoskeletal pain, headaches, and sports-injury recovery; as an ACC-registered osteopath, Nina's sessions are ACC-subsidised for accident-related injuries. Based in Hillmorton, our clinic welcomes patients from Wigram, Addington, Halswell, Cashmere and across greater Christchurch.

specialties.title: Pain Relief, Dry Needling & Recovery
specialties.description: Acupuncture is highly effective for both acute and chronic pain — through Traditional Chinese Medicine point selection, Western dry needling of muscular trigger points, or a blend of both. By stimulating specific points on the body, it triggers the release of natural pain-relieving chemicals, reduces inflammation, and improves circulation. Nina integrates dry needling with osteopathic treatment in the same session for back pain, neck pain, headaches, stubborn muscle spasm, and sports-injury recovery — particularly useful for the upper traps, suboccipitals, glutes, calves, and forearms. Min uses TCM-based acupuncture for chronic pain patterns that benefit from a constitutional approach. Whichever path you take, acupuncture offers a drug-free route to lasting relief.
specialties.alt: Close-up of an acupuncturist's hands inserting fine needles into a patient's shoulder

specialties.title: Stress, Anxiety & Sleep
specialties.description: In today's fast-paced world, stress and poor sleep take a significant toll on health. Acupuncture helps regulate the nervous system, lower cortisol levels, and promote a deep sense of calm. Regular treatment can improve sleep quality, reduce anxiety, and restore emotional balance — helping you feel grounded and resilient.
specialties.alt: Hand of a practitioner holding a smouldering moxibustion stick above a draped patient's lower back

specialties.title: Women's Health
specialties.description: Acupuncture has been used for centuries to support women's health across all life stages. From regulating menstrual cycles and easing period pain to managing pregnancy discomforts and navigating perimenopause and menopause, our practitioners provide gentle, holistic care tailored to your unique needs. Infertility acupuncture is one of our most sought-after specialties — research supports its role in improving ovarian function, regulating hormonal cycles, reducing stress, and enhancing uterine lining receptivity. Whether you are trying to conceive naturally or are undergoing IVF or other assisted reproductive treatments, acupuncture can be a powerful complement to your fertility journey, supporting both physical and emotional wellbeing throughout the process.
specialties.alt: Smiling middle-aged woman seated calmly in a bright modern clinic waiting area

learnMore.text: Learn more about fertility & IVF support

specialties.title: Digestive & Immune Health
specialties.description: A healthy gut is the foundation of overall wellbeing. Acupuncture can help regulate digestive function, reduce bloating and discomfort, and support conditions such as IBS, reflux, and nausea. It also plays a role in strengthening immune resilience and managing fatigue, helping your body maintain its natural balance.
specialties.alt: Patient and clinician in conversation across a desk with bowls of grains and a digestive-system anatomy poster on the wall

faq.question: Is acupuncture painful?
faq.answer: Most patients find acupuncture comfortable. The needles are very fine — finer than a hair — and any sensation is usually brief. Many people feel deeply relaxed during treatment.

faq.question: What does acupuncture treat?
faq.answer: Acupuncture is effective for chronic and acute pain, headaches and migraines, stress and anxiety, sleep difficulties, women's health concerns (including menstrual irregularities, fertility, and menopause), digestive complaints, and fatigue. Our practitioners blend traditional Chinese medicine with modern evidence-based approaches.

faq.question: What's the difference between acupuncture and dry needling?
faq.answer: Both use the same fine, sterile, single-use filiform needles, but they are framed differently. Traditional Chinese Medicine acupuncture selects points along meridians based on your overall pattern — sleep, digestion, menstrual cycle, energy, and the presenting complaint together. Dry needling (also called Western medical acupuncture) selects points anatomically — into a specific muscular trigger point, motor point, or peripheral nerve — with the immediate goal of releasing muscle spasm and dampening local pain. At Meridian, Min Jin leads our TCM acupuncture and Nina Hu leads our dry-needling work; you can choose the lens that fits your goal, or we will recommend one at your first visit.

faq.question: Which practitioner should I book — Min or Nina?
faq.answer: Book Min Jin if you are seeking Traditional Chinese Medicine — she holds a Bachelor of Acupuncture and Traditional Chinese Medicine, is registered with the Chinese Medicine Council of New Zealand, and works across women's health, fertility, sleep, stress, digestion, and constitutional patterns. Book Nina Hu if you are seeking dry needling or integrated osteopathy + acupuncture for musculoskeletal pain, sports injuries, headaches, or trigger-point release — she holds a Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture) and treats patients from Hillmorton, serving Halswell, Wigram, Addington and surrounding suburbs. If you are unsure, book either of them and we will redirect within the clinic if a different practitioner is a better fit.

faq.question: Can acupuncture help with fertility and IVF?
faq.answer: Yes. Research supports acupuncture's role in improving ovarian function, regulating hormonal cycles, reducing stress, and enhancing uterine lining receptivity. It can complement both natural conception and assisted reproductive treatments such as IVF.

faq.question: Is acupuncture or dry needling covered by ACC?
faq.answer: ACC funding for acupuncture at Meridian flows through Nina Hu's osteopath ACC contract — she's an ACC-registered osteopath who integrates dry needling within her consultations, so a single ACC-subsidised appointment covers both manual therapy and needle work for accident-related injuries. Min Jin's TCM acupuncture is private (CMCNZ-registered, not ACC-contracted). No referral needed — you can book either directly.

faq.question: What can I expect at my first dry-needling session?
faq.answer: Your first visit starts with a brief history and a physical assessment — Nina will ask about your pain pattern, what aggravates and eases it, your sleep, and any relevant injury history, then check movement, posture, and the muscles she suspects are involved. Treatment typically combines hands-on osteopathic technique with dry needling into specific trigger points: the needle insertion is brief and the most common sensation is a deep ache or muscle twitch that settles within seconds. Sessions are 40–60 minutes. Mild post-treatment soreness for 24–48 hours is normal; gentle movement and hydration help.

fertility.title: Acupuncture for Fertility & IVF Support
fertility.intro: Fertility acupuncture treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch supports patients trying to conceive naturally and those undergoing IVF, IUI, or other assisted reproductive treatments. Nina holds a Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture) and integrates osteopathy with traditional Chinese medicine — typically 8–12 weeks of weekly sessions before egg retrieval. No referral needed; you can book directly. Fertility-focused acupuncture is one of our most sought-after areas of care.

sections.title: How acupuncture supports fertility
sections.description: Research suggests acupuncture can improve ovarian function, regulate hormonal cycles, increase pelvic blood flow, support uterine lining receptivity, and lower the stress hormones that disrupt ovulation. Treatment is tailored to where you are in your cycle — follicular, ovulatory, luteal — and to your wider picture of sleep, digestion, and energy.
sections.alt: Close-up of fine acupuncture needles placed in a patient's hand resting on a treatment table in a softly lit clinic

sections.title: IVF cycle support — timing matters
sections.description: For IVF, the strongest-supported protocol is to begin acupuncture 8–12 weeks before egg retrieval, with weekly sessions through the down-regulation and stimulation phases. We add pre- and post-transfer treatments on the day of embryo transfer where possible, then continue weekly through the two-week wait. If you're starting an IVF cycle sooner than that, we can still help — we'll prioritise stimulation-phase and transfer-day support.
sections.alt: Person in a soft white knit jumper cradling a warm cup of herbal tea in both hands

sections.title: Nina's training and approach
sections.description: Nina trained as an osteopath (Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health, Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy) and completed a Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture). She has a particular clinical interest in women's health and integrates cranial osteopathic techniques and acupuncture in the same session to address pelvic alignment, autonomic balance, and hormonal rhythm. She works alongside your fertility specialist or GP, not in place of them.
sections.alt: Acupuncturist in a white clinical coat demonstrating careful fine-needle technique on a patient's hand

faq.question: When should I start acupuncture if I'm doing IVF?
faq.answer: Ideally 8–12 weeks before egg retrieval, with weekly sessions through down-regulation and stimulation. Pre- and post-transfer treatments on transfer day are added where the timing works, then weekly support continues through the two-week wait. If your cycle is already underway we can still help — we'll focus on the stimulation phase and transfer day.

faq.question: Can acupuncture help if I'm trying to conceive naturally?
faq.answer: Yes. Treatment is tailored to your menstrual cycle and wider picture — sleep, digestion, stress, and pelvic biomechanics. The aim is to regulate hormonal rhythm, support ovulation, and improve uterine lining receptivity. We typically recommend weekly sessions for at least three full cycles to give the body time to respond.

faq.question: Is fertility acupuncture covered by ACC?
faq.answer: No. ACC subsidises treatment for accident-related injuries only, so fertility and IVF support sits outside ACC cover. Our private (non-ACC) rate of $115–$120 applies. If you also have an ACC injury, that part of your care can still be claimed separately.

faq.question: What are Nina's credentials in fertility acupuncture?
faq.answer: Nina holds a Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health, a Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy, and a Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture). She is registered with the Osteopathic Council of New Zealand and has a particular clinical interest in women's health, integrating cranial osteopathic techniques with acupuncture for hormonal balance and pelvic health.

faq.question: Will you work alongside my fertility specialist?
faq.answer: Yes. Acupuncture is complementary to your medical fertility care, not a replacement for it. With your permission we communicate with your fertility clinic or GP to coordinate timing — particularly around stimulation, trigger, and transfer.

faq.question: Is acupuncture safe in early pregnancy?
faq.answer: Yes. Specific points are avoided in pregnancy and treatment is gentle. Acupuncture in the first trimester is sometimes used to support early pregnancy where there is a history of recurrent miscarriage — always coordinated with your medical team.

herbs.title: Herbal Medicine
herbs.intro: Herbal medicine treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch draws on Traditional Chinese Medicine combined with contemporary research. Our practitioners create personalised plant-based prescriptions for chronic pain and inflammation, digestive complaints, stress, sleep difficulties, low mood, fatigue, women's health (menstrual, fertility, menopause), and hormonal imbalance. No referral needed; you can book directly. Our Hillmorton-based practitioners see patients from Wigram, Addington, Halswell, Cashmere and surrounding Christchurch suburbs.

specialties.title: Pain & Inflammation
specialties.description: Herbal formulas have long been used to address pain and reduce inflammation throughout the body. Our practitioners select specific herbs known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, creating personalised blends that work alongside your other treatments to provide sustained relief from chronic pain, arthritis, and musculoskeletal conditions.
specialties.alt: Selection of dried Chinese herbs in wooden trays and marble bowls beside a stone mortar and pestle on a marble counter

specialties.title: Stress, Sleep & Mental Wellbeing
specialties.description: Adaptogenic and calming herbs have a profound ability to support the nervous system, reduce the impact of stress, and promote restful sleep. Our herbal prescriptions are tailored to your individual pattern — whether you're experiencing burnout, anxiety, insomnia, or low mood — to help restore your energy and emotional balance.
specialties.alt: Open wooden apothecary drawers filled with dried Chinese herbs, with ceramic storage jars on the shelf above

specialties.title: Digestive Health
specialties.description: Digestive health is central to overall wellness in Traditional Chinese Medicine. Herbal formulas can help regulate gut function, ease bloating, cramping, and discomfort, and support conditions such as IBS, reflux, and poor appetite. Our practitioners assess your individual presentation to create a formula that addresses the root cause, not just the symptoms.
specialties.alt: Wooden tray of varied dried Chinese herbs — including goji berries and reishi slices — beside pre-packaged formula sachets

specialties.title: Women's Health & Hormonal Balance
specialties.description: Herbal medicine is particularly powerful in supporting women's health throughout every life stage. From regulating menstrual cycles and supporting fertility to managing symptoms of perimenopause and menopause, our personalised herbal prescriptions work with your body's natural rhythms to restore hormonal balance and promote lasting wellbeing.
specialties.alt: Glass bottles of herbal infusions with chamomile flowers, fresh herbs and a pink rose alongside a bowl of dried rosebud tea

faq.question: What is Chinese herbal medicine?
faq.answer: Traditional Chinese herbal medicine uses carefully balanced plant-based formulas, prescribed individually to your constitution and presenting pattern. It works alongside acupuncture and osteopathy to support healing from the inside out.

faq.question: How are the herbs prescribed?
faq.answer: After a full case history and assessment, your practitioner creates a personalised formula tailored to your specific pattern and concern. You'll typically take the formula as a granule or liquid over several weeks, with the blend adjusted as your condition changes.

faq.question: Are Chinese herbs safe alongside other medications?
faq.answer: In most cases yes, but always tell your practitioner about every medication and supplement you take so they can prescribe safely. Our practitioners are trained to recognise and avoid herb-drug interactions, and will adjust or defer herbs when that's the safer call.

faq.question: What does herbal medicine help with?
faq.answer: Herbal formulas are used for chronic pain and inflammation, digestive complaints, stress, sleep difficulties, low mood, fatigue, women's health (menstrual, fertility, menopausal symptoms), and hormonal imbalances. It's particularly useful for slow-to-resolve or constitutional patterns.
